what: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: pTRAPPING R package provides streamlined, reproducible workflow analysing TRAP-seq PhosphoTRAP RNA-sequencing data. wraps statistical power edgeR limma behind compact set purpose-built functions take raw counts matrix publication-ready tables figures just lines code. TRAP-seq? Translating Ribosome Affinity Purification followed sequencing (TRAP-seq) captures mRNAs actively translated specific cell type, tagged transgenic ribosomal subunit. key comparison IP fraction (immunoprecipitated, actively translated transcripts) INPUT fraction (total RNA — transcripts). Genes significantly enriched IP relative INPUT translated tagged cell type. PhosphoTRAP adds activity-marking phosphorylation step, recently activated neurons captured — combining cell-type specificity behavioural state information single experiment.

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Throughout README simulate mouse TRAP-seq experiment studying pair-bonding preoptic area (POA) hypothalamus — brain region well-known role social behaviour. Mice divided two groups: nb – non-bonded control mice pb – pair-bonded mice (48 h co-housing partner) group 3 animals (biological replicates). animal contributes one IP one INPUT library, giving 12 samples total. column-naming convention <treatment><replicate><fraction> (e.g. nb1INPUT, pb2IP) lets ptrap_de() parse sample metadata automatically — sample_df needed. first column holds gene identifiers; remaining 12 columns raw counts. Snap25 (neuronal) already shows high IP counts groups, Oxtr (pair-bonding marker) shows balanced IP/INPUT — enriched pb.
code: library(pTRAPPING) library(ggplot2)  set.seed(42)  n_genes  <- 2000 gene_ids <- paste0("Gene", seq_len(n_genes))  # ── Replace early positions with real mouse gene names ────────────────────── # Neuronal markers — expected to be enriched in IP in BOTH groups neuronal   <- c("Snap25", "Rbfox3", "Camk2a", "Vglut1", "Syn1",                 "Map2",   "Nrgn",   "Bdnf",   "Pvalb",  "Sst") # Pair-bonding markers — enriched in IP only in pb pb_markers <- c("Oxtr", "Avpr1a", "Fos", "Arc", "Egr1") # Glial markers — NOT enriched in neuronal IP (control transcripts) glial      <- c("Gfap", "Aldh1l1", "S100b", "Cx3cr1", "Tmem119")  gene_ids[seq_along(neuronal)]                                       <- neuronal gene_ids[length(neuronal) + seq_along(pb_markers)]                  <- pb_markers gene_ids[length(neuronal) + length(pb_markers) + seq_along(glial)]  <- glial  # ── True IP / INPUT enrichment ratios ─────────────────────────────────────── base_enrich        <- rep(1.0, n_genes) base_enrich[1:10]  <- 8   # neuronal markers — constitutively enriched in both groups base_enrich[11:15] <- 1   # pair-bonding markers — baseline (no enrichment in nb)  pb_extra           <- rep(1.0, n_genes) pb_extra[11:15]    <- 8   # pair-bonding markers strongly enriched only in pb  # ── Helper: simulate one IP/INPUT pair for all genes ──────────────────────── sim_pair <- function(enrich, mu = 200, size = 10, sd_noise = 0.15) {   input <- rnbinom(n_genes, mu = mu, size = size)   ip    <- pmax(round(input * enrich * exp(rnorm(n_genes, 0, sd_noise))), 0L)   list(input = input, ip = ip) }  # ── Build counts data frame with auto-parseable column names ──────────────── cols <- lapply(1:3, function(i) {   nb <- sim_pair(base_enrich)   pb <- sim_pair(base_enrich * pb_extra)   setNames(     data.frame(nb$input, nb$ip, pb$input, pb$ip),     c(paste0("nb", i, "INPUT"), paste0("nb", i, "IP"),       paste0("pb", i, "INPUT"), paste0("pb", i, "IP"))   ) })  counts_mat <- data.frame(Gene = gene_ids, do.call(cbind, cols))  # Preview — neuronal marker, pair-bonding marker, glial marker counts_mat[c(1, 11, 16), 1:7] #>      Gene nb1INPUT nb1IP pb1INPUT pb1IP nb2INPUT nb2IP #> 1  Snap25      274  2231      150   910      229  1467 #> 11   Oxtr      217   260       83   630      290   333 #> 16   Gfap      151   117      143   123      222   307

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: sample_df omitted, ptrap_de() reads treatment, replicate number, fraction directly column names. Providing treatment_name tells function group analyse: 1 neuronal markers (Snap25, Camk2a, etc.) enriched IP groups. pair-bonding markers (Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1) appear pb result — exactly expected activity-dependent transcripts pair-bonded neurons.
code: res_nb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "LRT"        # edgeR likelihood ratio test ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes in nb head(res_nb[res_nb$diffexpressed != "NO",             c("Gene", "logFC", "LR", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   Gene   logFC    LR    PValue       FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Sst     2.95  695. 3.27e-153 6.54e-150 UP            #> 2 Syn1    3.00  693. 1.10e-152 1.10e-149 UP            #> 3 Snap25  2.90  656. 1.40e-144 9.31e-142 UP            #> 4 Vglut1  3.01  631. 3.12e-139 1.56e-136 UP            #> 5 Bdnf    2.92  624. 1.13e-137 4.50e-135 UP            #> 6 Rbfox3  2.79  616. 5.50e-136 1.83e-133 UP res_pb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes in pb head(res_pb[res_pb$diffexpressed != "NO",             c("Gene", "logFC", "LR", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   Gene  logFC    LR    PValue       FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Egr1   3.23  629. 6.88e-139 1.38e-135 UP            #> 2 Map2   3.12  600. 1.63e-132 1.63e-129 UP            #> 3 Arc    3.13  591. 1.60e-130 1.07e-127 UP            #> 4 Syn1   2.98  550. 1.36e-121 6.78e-119 UP            #> 5 Sst    2.94  543. 4.18e-120 1.67e-117 UP            #> 6 Nrgn   2.99  542. 5.61e-120 1.87e-117 UP

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Note "paired.ttest": GLM-based methods ("LRT" / "QLF") estimate gene-wise dispersion data work best ≥ 4 replicates. n = 3, dispersion estimates unreliable. paired t-test follows Tan et al. (2016) exactly: “p value gene calculated paired t test input immunoprecipitated RPKM values three experimental repeats.” Set norm.method = "RPKM" (supply gene.length) reproduce original analysis. logFC log₂(mean_IP / mean_INPUT).
code: # Quasi-likelihood F-test — more conservative than LRT res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # limma-voom — accounts for mean-variance trend with precision weights res_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "voom" )  # Paired t-test — recommended for real PhosphoTRAP data with n = 3 animals # Follows the approach in Tan et al. 2016 (Cell 167, 47-59) res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest" )

what: Long-format paired data with return_long = TRUE
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: return_long = TRUE ("paired.ttest") returns per-gene, per-animal paired table used internally — useful plotting individual data points performing additional quality checks:
code: res_long <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # DE results tibble head(res_long$results[, c("Gene", "logFC", "t_statistic", "PValue", "FDR")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   Gene      logFC t_statistic    PValue    FDR #>   <chr>     <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 Gene1833  0.142       251.  0.0000159 0.0317 #> 2 Pvalb     2.91         38.6 0.000670  0.504  #> 3 Gene68    0.125        36.4 0.000755  0.504  #> 4 Gene51    0.132        25.1 0.00158   0.653  #> 5 Gene1017 -0.118       -23.4 0.00182   0.653  #> 6 Gene1529  0.196        22.6 0.00196   0.653  # Per-gene, per-animal paired table (showing Snap25): # ip_count / input_count are in the norm.method scale; FE = IP/INPUT per animal res_long$long_data[res_long$long_data$Gene == "Snap25", ] #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   Gene   block ip_count input_count    FE #>   <chr>  <chr>    <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Snap25 1        5522.        679.  8.12 #> 2 Snap25 2        3580.        556.  6.43 #> 3 Snap25 3        3462.        440.  7.86

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Adjust lfc_threshold fdr_threshold control strict DE classification :
code: # Relaxed: any enrichment above 0.5 log2FC at FDR < 0.10 res_relaxed <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT",   lfc_threshold  = 0.5,   fdr_threshold  = 0.10 ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  table(res_relaxed$diffexpressed) #>  #>   NO   UP  #> 1984   16

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Set kable.= TRUE get formatted HTML table top genes — ideal Quarto / R Markdown reports:
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "LRT",   kable.out      = TRUE,   ngenes.out     = 10 )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: classic volcano plot one treatment condition, significant genes colour-coded labelled:  Customise colours thresholds:  Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1 appear pb plot — consistent known roles pair-bond formation neuronal immediate-early gene expression.
code: ptrap_volcano(   res_nb,   title = "Non-bonded (nb) — POA" ) ptrap_volcano(   res_pb,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   colors        = c("UP" = "#E69F00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9"),   title         = "Pair-bonded (pb) — POA" )

what: ptrap_volcano2() — dual scatter comparing two treatments
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: ptrap_volcano2() overlays treatment conditions single scatter plot. axis shows log₂ fold-change (IP / INPUT) one group, genes colour-coded DE status across conditions:  read plot * Top-right quadrant: enriched IP groups → constitutive neuronal translation (e.g. Snap25, Camk2a, Rbfox3). * Bottom-right: enriched pb → activity-dependent translation pair-bonded animals (Oxtr, Fos, Arc, Egr1, Avpr1a). * Top-left: enriched nb → non-bonded-specific. * dotted diagonal identity line — equal enrichment groups. Customise colours thresholds:
code: ptrap_volcano2(   res_nb, res_pb,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   title         = "POA — nb vs pb" ) ptrap_volcano2(   res_nb, res_pb,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   title         = "POA — nb vs pb",   colors        = c(     "DE in both"   = "#D55E00",     "DE only nb"   = "#0072B2",     "DE only pb"   = "#009E73"   ) )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: "voom" applies limma-voom pipeline: counts converted log-CPM precision weights estimated mean-variance trend fitting IP vs INPUT linear model. particularly useful library sizes vary substantially across samples, weights -weight unreliable observations automatically. output structure "LRT" / "QLF" results (Gene, logFC, FDR, diffexpressed, …), drops straight ptrap_volcano():
code: res_nb_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   treatment_name = "nb",   test_method    = "voom" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP  # Top enriched genes head(res_nb_voom[res_nb_voom$diffexpressed != "NO",                  c("Gene", "logFC", "t", "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   Gene   logFC     t   PValue      FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Sst     2.95  28.9 1.31e-55 1.85e-52 UP            #> 2 Syn1    3.00  28.8 1.85e-55 1.85e-52 UP            #> 3 Vglut1  3.02  27.8 6.39e-54 4.26e-51 UP            #> 4 Snap25  2.90  27.6 1.57e-53 7.84e-51 UP            #> 5 Bdnf    2.91  27.2 5.11e-53 2.04e-50 UP            #> 6 Map2    2.96  27.1 7.69e-53 2.56e-50 UP ptrap_volcano(   res_nb_voom,   title = "Non-bonded (nb) — POA [voom]" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: unpaired t-test method follows PhosphoTRAP approach Knight et al. (2012): per-gene fold enrichments (FE = IP / INPUT) computed every animal group, Welch t-test used compare log₂(FE) two treatment groups. right choice want ask: “IP enrichment different group group B?” rather testing IP vs INPUT within single group. neither treatment_name control_name supplied counts matrix contains exactly two groups, assigned automatically (alphabetically; first = control, second = treatment): Interpretation: diff_FE = mean_FE_pb / mean_FE_nb — ratio mean IP/INPUT fold enrichments treatment control. logFC log2(diff_FE). Genes logFC > 0 enriched pair-bonded group. Pair-bonding markers (Oxtr, Avpr1a, Fos, Arc, Egr1) show genes whose neuronal IP enrichment higher pb nb:
code: # Auto-assigns: nb = control, pb = treatment res_unpaired <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat  = counts_mat,   test_method = "unpaired.ttest" ) #> Auto-parsed sample metadata from column names. #>   Treatments : nb, pb #>   Blocks     : 1, 2, 3 #>   Fractions  : INPUT, IP #> Auto-assigned treatments alphabetically: control = 'nb', treatment = 'pb'. #> Low-power warning ('unpaired.ttest'): 'pb' has n = 3 and 'nb' has n = 3 replicates. #>   A Welch t-test with 3 replicates per group yields only 2 degree(s) of freedom per group, #>   making BH correction over 2000 genes highly conservative -- few or no genes may reach #>   significance at the default thresholds. Consider: #>     * relaxing fdr_threshold (e.g. 0.10 or 0.20) or lfc_threshold; #>     * increasing biological replicates (n >= 4 recommended); #>     * using test_method = 'LRT', 'QLF', 'voom', or 'deseq', which #>       borrow information across genes to improve power at small n.  # Results include mean FE per group and their ratio (diff_FE) head(res_unpaired[res_unpaired$diffexpressed != "NO",                   c("Gene", "logFC", "diff_FE",                     "mean_FE_nb", "mean_FE_pb",                     "PValue", "FDR", "diffexpressed")]) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   Gene  logFC diff_FE mean_FE_nb mean_FE_pb    PValue    FDR diffexpressed #>   <chr> <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl> <chr>         #> 1 Egr1   3.24    9.47      1.00        9.48 0.0000166 0.0210 UP            #> 2 Oxtr   2.89    7.42      1.12        8.28 0.0000287 0.0210 UP            #> 3 Arc    3.14    8.80      0.997       8.78 0.0000408 0.0210 UP            #> 4 Fos    2.99    7.92      1.03        8.13 0.0000420 0.0210 UP ptrap_volcano(   res_unpaired,   title = "pb vs nb — POA [unpaired t-test]" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: complex designs — multiple brain regions, custom blocking variables, non-standard column naming — supply sample_df directly:
code: # Example: two brain regions, two treatments sample_df <- data.frame(   sample    = c("POA_nb1_IP", "POA_nb1_INPUT", "POA_pb1_IP", "POA_pb1_INPUT",                 "AMY_nb1_IP", "AMY_nb1_INPUT", "AMY_pb1_IP", "AMY_pb1_INPUT"),   region    = rep(c("POA", "AMY"), each = 4),   treatment = rep(c("nb", "nb", "pb", "pb"), 2),   block     = rep("1", 8),   fraction  = rep(c("IP", "INPUT", "IP", "INPUT"), 2) )  res_poa_pb <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = my_counts,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = my_gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   region_col     = "region",   treatment_col  = "treatment",   block_col      = "block",   fraction_col   = "fraction",   test_method    = "paired.ttest" )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: use pTRAPPING research, please cite: Rodríguez, C. (2024). pTRAPPING: Suite Functions Analyses PhosphoTRAP Data R. https://github.com/laurenoconnelllab/pTRAPPING paired t-test method (test_method = "paired.ttest"): Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47–59. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 unpaired t-test method (test_method = "unpaired.ttest"): Knight, Z.., Tan, K., Birsoy, K., Schmidt, S., Garrison, J.L., Wysocki, R.W., Emiliano, ., Ekstrand, M.., Bhatt, D.L. (2012). Molecular profiling activated neurons phosphorylated ribosome capture. Cell 151, 1126–1137. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2012.10.039

what: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Compares IP vs INPUT fractions specified treatment condition using one six statistical approaches (see test_method). sample_df gene_ids optional: function can derive sample metadata automatically column names counts_mat, gene identifiers first column. "LRT" "QLF" use edgeR's GLM framework multi-factor design (~ fraction + block), accounting paired animal structure via blocking variable. methods automatically apply edgeR's TMM (trimmed mean M values) normalization via normLibSizes(), adjusts effective library sizes (count matrix ) use GLM offsets. norm.method applicable ignored. Recommended 4 replicates raw count data. "deseq" uses DESeq2 pipeline multi-factor design (~ block + fraction; fraction goes last results() auto-tests ). DESeq2 applies median ratios normalization internally via DESeq(). norm.method applicable ignored. Recommended prefer negative-binomial shrinkage estimators DESeq2 edgeR. Important: default, logFC maximum likelihood estimate (MLE) results(), can noisy large magnitude low-count genes. Set shrink.lfc = TRUE apply empirical Bayes LFC shrinkage via lfcShrink() (requires apeglm package), strongly recommended ranking genes producing volcano plots publication. "voom" uses limma-voom pipeline paired design (~ fraction + block) empirical Bayes moderation. Voom internally transforms counts log2-CPM (using TMM-adjusted library sizes) derives precision weights mean-variance trend; two steps integral method. norm.method applicable ignored. "paired.ttest" runs per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT values across experimental repeats, following Tan et al. (2016) Cell 167, 47-59 doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 : "p value gene calculated paired t-test input immunoprecipitated RPKM values three experimental repeats." count scale test controlled norm.method (Tan et al. used "RPKM"; three options valid; default "CPM"). logFC reported \(\log_2(\bar{\mathrm{IP}} / \bar{\mathrm{INPUT}})\). Recommended PhosphoTRAP experiments 3 replicates, GLM-based dispersion estimates unreliable. "unpaired.ttest" compares fold enrichments (IP/INPUT) two treatment groups using Welch (unpaired) t-test per-animal log2(FE) values. Use test whether ribosomal association differs conditions. Requires exactly two treatments; use treatment_name control_name specify . count scale controlled norm.method (default "CPM"; three options valid).

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat,   sample_df = NULL,   gene_ids = NULL,   region_name = NULL,   treatment_name = NULL,   control_name = NULL,   sample_col = "sample",   fraction_col = "fraction",   block_col = "tube",   region_col = NULL,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   ip_level = "IP",   input_level = "INPUT",   covariates = NULL,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   test_method = c("LRT", "QLF", "paired.ttest", "unpaired.ttest", "voom", "deseq"),   norm.method = c("CPM", "RPKM", "mratios", "none"),   gene.length = NULL,   prior.count = 1,   shrink.lfc = FALSE,   return_long = FALSE,   ngenes.out = 20,   genes.filter = NULL,   kable.out = FALSE,   filter = TRUE )

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: counts_mat counts matrix one two formats: Matrix – numeric, genes x samples; column names sample IDs; gene IDs rownames supplied via gene_ids. Data frame / tibble – first column character vector gene IDs; remaining columns numeric counts sample names column names. sample_df = NULL, column names must follow naming convention described Automatic column-name parsing section. sample_df Optional data frame sample metadata. NULL (default), metadata parsed automatically column names counts_mat. provided, arguments sample_col, fraction_col, block_col, region_col, treatment_col specify columns use (falling back defaults names match). gene_ids Optional character vector gene identifiers corresponding rows counts_mat. NULL (default), gene IDs extracted first column counts_mat (data frame) rownames(counts_mat) (matrix). region_name brain region subset analyse (e.g., "POA"). region_col NULL (default) region_name supplied, function automatically scans sample_df non-structural column whose values include region_name, uses region_col exactly one column found (message shown). Can NULL data contain single region. Auto-parsing mode (sample_df = NULL): region_name additionally used column-name parsing extract region token directly column name (e.g., "POA" b1inputPOA). See Automatic column-name parsing section details. treatment_name treatment condition whose samples subsetted IP vs INPUT comparison (e.g., "pb" analyse pair-bonded samples). "unpaired.ttest", numerator condition differential fold enrichment (DFE = mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control). NULL (default), value derived automatically sample_df (parsed column names sample_df = NULL): single treatment found function proceeds silently; multiple treatments found error asks user specify one analyse. "unpaired.ttest" exactly two treatments treatment_name control_name NULL, assignments made alphabetically (first = control, second = treatment) message. control_name reference / control treatment used "unpaired.ttest". Serves denominator computing differential fold enrichment (DFE = mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control). NULL (default), function auto-detects control non-treatment_name group exactly two treatments present. sample_col Name column sample_df whose values match column names counts_mat. Ignored sample_df = NULL (auto-set internally). Default "sample". fraction_col Name column sample_df distinguishes IP INPUT fractions. Default "fraction". block_col Name column sample_df used blocking / pairing variable (e.g., individual animal tube). "LRT" / "QLF", "deseq", "voom" enters design matrix; "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest" aligns animal's IP INPUT. Default "tube". region_col Name column sample_df containing brain region labels (e.g., "BrainRegion"). NULL (default) region_name supplied, column auto-detected (see region_name). Set explicitly auto-detection ambiguous prefer explicit. Leave NULL data contain one brain region. treatment_col Name column sample_df containing treatment labels. Default "Treatment". ip_level value fraction_col identifies IP fraction. Default "IP". input_level value fraction_col identifies INPUT fraction (reference level). Default "INPUT". covariates Optional character vector covariate names include design formula. Default NULL. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR (adjusted p-value) allowed classify gene differentially expressed. Default 0.05. test_method Statistical method use. One : "LRT" – edgeR likelihood ratio test via glmFit + glmLRT (default). TMM normalisation applied internally; norm.method applicable. Suitable >= 4 replicates raw count data. "QLF" – edgeR quasi-likelihood F-test via glmQLFit + glmQLFTest. TMM normalisation applied internally; norm.method applicable. conservative "LRT". "deseq" – DESeq2 pipeline multi-factor design (~ block + fraction). Median ratios normalisation applied internally DESeq(); norm.method applicable. Results obtained via results(dds, name = "fraction_IP_vs_INPUT"). logFC MLE default; use shrink.lfc = TRUE shrunken estimates. "voom" – limma-voom pipeline paired design (~ fraction + block) empirical Bayes moderation via lmFit + eBayes + topTable. Log2-CPM transformation voom precision weights applied internally; norm.method applicable. "paired.ttest" – per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT values across replicates, following Tan et al. (2016). count scale controlled norm.method (default "CPM"; Tan et al. used "RPKM"). Best suited n = 3 replicates. P-values adjusted BH. "unpaired.ttest" – per-gene Welch unpaired t-test comparing log2(FE) values two treatment groups. count scale controlled norm.method (default "CPM"). Requires exactly two treatments; use treatment_name control_name specify group . norm.method Count normalisation method used t-test branches ("paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest"). Ignored "LRT", "QLF", "voom", "deseq", handle normalisation internally (message shown explicitly supply norm.method methods). Default "CPM". One : "CPM" (default) – counts per million, computed edgeR's TMM-adjusted effective library sizes (lib.size x norm.factors) via edgeR::cpm(). TMM adjusts effective library sizes rather count matrix directly, CPM values reflect sequencing depth TMM normalisation. Suitable comparisons replicates sample group. "RPKM" – reads per kilobase per million: CPM divided gene length kilobases, via edgeR::rpkm(). Requires gene.length. Suitable within-sample comparisons genes (scale used Tan et al. 2016 PhosphoTRAP). recommended -sample comparisons. "mratios" – DESeq2 median ratios normalisation, computed via estimateSizeFactors() retrieved counts(dds, normalized = TRUE). Unlike CPM, method directly rescales count matrix sample-specific size factors, making suitable -sample comparisons. Use want DESeq2-style normalisation t-test without running full DESeq2 pipeline. "none" – normalisation applied; count matrix used supplied. Intended pre-normalised matrices (e.g., TPM, FPKM, user-normalised values) additional normalisation step redundant distorting. gene.length Named numeric vector gene lengths base pairs (names must match gene IDs). Required norm.method = "RPKM"; ignored otherwise. Default NULL. prior.count small count added IP INPUT values computing log2 ratios (FE = (IP + prior.count) / (INPUT + prior.count)), avoid log(0). Default 1. Set 0 values already normalised guaranteed positive. Used "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest". shrink.lfc Logical. used test_method = "deseq". TRUE, log2 fold changes shrunk using empirical Bayes estimation via DESeq2::lfcShrink(type = "apeglm"), requires apeglm package. Shrunken LFCs reliable ranking genes visualisation pull noisy, high-variance estimates (typically low-count genes) towards zero. P-values FDR affected – always come results(). Default FALSE (MLE fold changes returned), TRUE strongly recommended result used figure table. return_long Logical. used test_method "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest". TRUE, returns named list $results (DE tibble) $long_data (per-gene, per-animal table used test). Default FALSE. ngenes.Number top genes (sorted p-value) include output kable.= TRUE. Ignored genes.filter supplied. Default 20. genes.filter Optional character vector gene names (matching Gene column output) retain. supplied, listed genes returned ngenes.ignored. Default NULL. kable.Logical. TRUE, returns kableExtra HTML table top ngenes.genes instead full tibble. Default FALSE.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: kable.= FALSE (default), tibble one row per gene sorted p-value. Columns depend test_method: "LRT" / "QLF": Gene, logFC, logCPM, LR F, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed ("", "", ""). "deseq": Gene, baseMean, logFC (MLE log2FoldChange default; empirical Bayes shrunken estimate shrink.lfc = TRUE), lfcSE, stat (Wald statistic; present MLE, absent shrink.lfc = TRUE apeglm replaces posterior estimate), PValue, FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg results(); unaffected shrinkage), treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. Genes NA p-values (low-count outliers flagged DESeq2) retained sorted bottom. "paired.ttest": always returns named list two components: $results — tibble Gene, logFC (\(\log_2(\bar{\mathrm{IP}} / \bar{\mathrm{INPUT}})\)), t_statistic, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. $fe — wide tibble Gene plus one FE_<block> column per animal, containing per-animal linear IP/INPUT fold enrichment values used test. $long_data — (return_long = TRUE) per-gene, per-animal tibble columns Gene, block column, ip_count, input_count, FE. "unpaired.ttest": Gene, logFC (log2(mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control)), diff_FE, mean_FE_<treatment>, mean_FE_<control>, t_statistic, df (Welch degrees freedom), PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. return_long = TRUE, returns named list: $results $long_data (per-gene, per-animal, per-treatment tibble columns Gene, block column, treatment column, FE, log2_FE). "voom": Gene, logFC, AveExpr, t, B, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. kable.= TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes.genes.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Perform differential expression analysis PhosphoTRAP data using edgeR, paired t-test, unpaired t-test, voom/limma

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: sample_df = NULL, column names counts_mat (excluding first, gene-ID column) parsed build sample metadata automatically. column name must encode three pieces information, order combination separators (_, -, ., space) separator : Treatment One letters identifying experimental group. Replicate number digit identifying biological replicate. Fraction ip_level input_level (case-insensitive); "" also accepted short alias INPUT fraction. Valid column name examples (default ip_level = "IP", input_level = "INPUT"): Region-aware parsing: region_name also supplied sample_df = NULL, parser uses region_name identify strip brain-region token column name. Matching case-insensitive extracted region stored region column auto-built sample metadata (used usual region-filtering step). Column names contain supplied region_name retain unrecognised token part treatment string; samples automatically excluded function filters treatment_name. Region-aware examples (region_name = "POA", ip_level = "IP", input_level = "INPUT"):

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47-59. doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 Love, M.., Huber, W., Anders, S. (2014). Moderated estimation fold change dispersion RNA-seq data DESeq2. Genome Biology 15, 550. doi:10.1186/s13059-014-0550-8

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## ---- Option A: simplest call -- auto-parse from column names --------------- # counts_mat is a data frame where: #   col 1      = gene IDs #   col 2+     = samples named like "b1input", "b1ip", "nb2input", etc. counts <- read.table("counts.txt", header = TRUE)  # single treatment in the matrix -- treatment_name auto-detected res <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, test_method = "paired.ttest")  # multiple treatments -- specify which one to analyze res_b <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, treatment_name = "b")  ## ---- Option B: provide sample_df explicitly (original workflow) ----------- res_lrt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # quasi-likelihood F-test res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # paired t-test -- also return long-format paired table res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) res_pt$results    # DE tibble res_pt$long_data  # per-gene, per-animal pairs  # unpaired t-test -- compare fold enrichment between two treatments res_upt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   treatment_name = "pb",   control_name   = "nb",   test_method    = "unpaired.ttest" )  # voom/limma pipeline res_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "voom" )  # paired t-test with CPM normalization res_cpm <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   norm.method    = "CPM" ) } # }

what: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: Takes single tibble produced ptrap_de() returns classic volcano plot (logFC x-axis, -log_base(p) y-axis). Non-significant genes shown grey; genes classified "" "" highlighted distinct fill colours. Gene labels added via ggrepel::geom_text_repel() genes supplied genes.annot.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: ptrap_volcano(   de_result,   fdr = TRUE,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   log_base = 10,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   genes.annot = NULL,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: de_result tibble returned ptrap_de(). test_method = "paired.ttest", pass $results component. fdr Logical. TRUE (default), uses FDR; FALSE, uses PValue. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change. Default 1. fdr_threshold P-value cutoff. Default 0.05. log_base Numeric. Base logarithm p-value axis. Default 10 (-log10, current behaviour). Use 2 -log2(p) Tan et al. (2016). Must positive number 1. gene_col Column name gene identifiers. Default "Gene". treatment_col Column name treatment label. Default "treatment". region_col Column name brain region label. Default "BrainRegion". colors Named vector mapping "" "" colours. Default colourblind-friendly: "#D55E00" (), "#0072B2" (). point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity highlighted points. Default 0.7. genes.annot Character vector gene names label. Default NULL. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Default 20. title Plot title. Auto-generated region treatment NULL.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: DE classification recomputed inside function logFC, chosen p-value column, supplied thresholds.

title: Single volcano plot for TRAP-seq or RNA-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default: -log10(FDR) ptrap_volcano(res)  # Raw p-values, log2 scale -- as in Tan et al. 2016 ptrap_volcano(res, fdr = FALSE, log_base = 2) } # }

what: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: Takes two tibbles produced ptrap_de() — one per treatment condition, brain region — joins gene, classifies gene according differential expression status condition, returns scatter plot logFC\(_{\text{treatment 1}}\) vs logFC\(_{\text{treatment 2}}\). Significant genes highlighted labelled; threshold lines drawn ±lfc_threshold axes.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: ptrap_volcano2(   de_result_1,   de_result_2,   fdr = TRUE,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   genes.annot = NULL,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL )

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: de_result_1 tibble returned ptrap_de() first treatment condition (plotted y-axis). de_result_2 tibble returned ptrap_de() second treatment condition (plotted x-axis). fdr Logical. TRUE (default), significance assessed using FDR column (BH-adjusted p-values). FALSE, raw PValue column used instead. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed given condition (also sets vertical horizontal threshold lines). Default 1. fdr_threshold P-value cutoff used classify genes DE. Applied FDR fdr = TRUE PValue fdr = FALSE. Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers result tibbles. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label result tibbles. Used auto-generate axis labels DE class names. Default "treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used set default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping DE class colour. Names must "DE ", "DE <t1>", "DE <t2>", <t1> / <t2> treatment names found data. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used. point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity coloured (significant) points. Default 0.7. genes.annot Character vector gene names label plot via ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Names must match values column specified gene_col; error raised names found. Default NULL (labels). max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels allowed hidden. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), brain region name extracted de_result_1 used.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
text: Significance can assessed using either FDR-adjusted p-values (fdr = TRUE, default) raw p-values (fdr = FALSE). cases cutoff fdr_threshold classification computed inside function supplied thresholds.

title: Dual volcano plot comparing two treatment conditions from TRAP-seq DE results — ptrap_volcano2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res_pb  <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "pb") res_sol <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "sol")  # Default plot — FDR on both axes ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol)  # Use raw p-values for the DE classification ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol, fdr = FALSE)  # Custom thresholds, title and colours ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol,                lfc_threshold = 0.5,                fdr_threshold = 0.1,                title         = "POA: pb vs sol",                colors        = c("DE in both"  = "#D55E00",                                  "DE only pb"  = "#0072B2",                                  "DE only sol" = "#49a15f")) } # }
